title: 5 players the Celtics could target this offseason outside of Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Kawhi Leonard is a potential trade target for the Celtics, known for his versatility and defensive skills.
Boston could pursue LeBron James, an unrestricted free agent, offering experience and playmaking as a fit for their lineup.
John Collins, Jaxson Hayes, and Coby White are considered for their roles to bolster Celtic's forward and center positions.

title: 2 local college baseball programs advance to NCAA World Series
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Bentley University advanced to the NCAA Division II World Series for the first time, defeating Molloy University in a three-game series.
Endicott College secured its fourth consecutive NCAA Division III World Series spot by defeating Salve Regina University in the Super Regionals.
Bentley's Stan DeMartinis III scored the game-winning run on a wild pitch, while Endicott's AJ Hamm, TJ Liponis, and Brenden Walsh hit home runs.

title: Celtics reportedly plan to work out UConn basketball star ahead of NBA Draft
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Celtics will reportedly hold a pre-draft workout for UConn star Alex Karaban next week.
Karaban, a Southborough native, is ranked No. 32 on ESPN’s top 100 draft prospects list.
Karaban led UConn with 1,880 career points and won two NCAA championships during his collegiate career.

title: Review & setlist: Charlie Puth turns MGM Music Hall into a masterclass in musicianship
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Charlie Puth performed at MGM Music Hall in Fenway, blending jazz and pop in his 'Whatever's Clever! World Tour'.
Puth's setlist included new songs and older hits, with standout performances of 'Beat Yourself Up' and 'One Call Away'.
The concert showcased Puth's musicianship, featuring his piano improvisations and engaging discussions on music theory with the audience.

title: Review and setlist: Jakob Dylan’s choice to channel Tom Petty pays off at Beverly Wallflowers show
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: The Wallflowers performed their 1996 album 'Bringing Down the Horse' in full at The Cabot in Beverly.
Jakob Dylan led the band in a set following 'Horse' with Tom Petty's 'Long After Dark' album.
The concert ended with hit encores including Petty's 'Refugee' and 'The Waiting', delighting the sold-out crowd.

title: Here’s where people are moving — and leaving — in Mass., according to new U.S. Census data
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston saw the largest population decline in Massachusetts, losing 1,338 residents last year, continuing a post-pandemic trend.
Everett and Worcester gained the most residents, with increases of 2,362 and 1,916 respectively, according to U.S. Census data.
Massachusetts' population grew by 15,500 last year, a 0.2% increase, significantly slower than previous years' growth rates.

title: These were the Mass. companies on the new Forbes list of largest family businesses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Fidelity Investments and Demoulas Super Markets named among America's largest family businesses by Forbes.
Fidelity, led by the Johnson family, ranks 14th with $37.7 billion in annual revenue.
Demoulas Super Markets, fully owned by the Demoulas family, ranks 69th with $8 billion annually.

title: UMass Amherst launches major housing revitalization project
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: UMass Amherst partners with American Campus Communities for a major housing overhaul to modernize and expand campus amenities.
The redevelopment will address housing shortages by renovating or replacing older buildings in phases while maintaining student occupancy.
The project aligns with statewide priorities to improve housing affordability, as highlighted by Gov. Maura Healey's administration.

title: Review & Setlist: Lucinda Williams brings protest songs, fighting spirit to Medford
author: Terence Cawley
bullets: Lucinda Williams performed at Chevalier Theatre in Medford, focusing on protest songs from her latest album 'World's Gone Wrong'.
Williams, unable to play guitar live post-stroke, delivered songs with vocal strength and protest-driven rhetoric.
Her setlist included covers of Bob Marley, Memphis Minnie, and Neil Young, emphasizing American roots and protest themes.

title: Twins unload on Justin Slaten as Red Sox lose 8-6 at Fenway Park
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Justin Slaten struggled, allowing four runs and two home runs, as the Red Sox fell 8-6 to the Twins.
Payton Tolle recorded nine strikeouts before being pulled after the sixth inning with the Red Sox leading.
Boston's offense, scoring six runs, was overshadowed by the Twins' performance, resulting in a series deficit.
